data.title: Oakmark Fund (Investor Class) adds ADBE, NFLX; exits DE, APA among Q1 moves
data.description:
Oakmark Fund (Investor Class) outperformed its benchmark, the S&P 500 Index, for the first quarter.

The fund, in its quarterly letter, stated that the largest contributors to performance were the energy and materials sectors, while financials and information technology were the largest detractors.

ConocoPhillips (COP), Targa Resources (TRGP), and Phillips 66 (PSX) were the top contributors during the quarter, while Salesforce (CRM), Capital One Financial (COF), and IQVIA Holdings (IQV) were among the top detractors.

During the quarter, the fund initiated new positions in Accenture (ACN), Adobe (ADBE), Marsh & McLennan (MMC), Netflix (NFLX), Raymond James Financial (RJF), Roper Technologies (ROP), Synchrony Financial (SYF), and Sysco (SYY).

On the other hand, the fund exited APA Corporation (APA), Baxter International (BAX), Celanese (CE), and Deere & Company (DE).

Source: Quarterly fund letter

data.title: Tax Day: What you need to know about filing, paying, requesting extension
data.description:
Today is Tax Day, the yearly deadline for most Americans to file their federal income tax returns for the 2025 tax year. Whether you're ready to submit or scrambling for more time, here's what you need to know before the clock runs out.

Deadline is today

The IRS deadline to file federal income tax returns is April 15, as the agency’s website says. If filing online, taxes must be submitted electronically before midnight in your time zone. If filing by paper, your return must be postmarked today.

What investors need to know

Tax Day carries two portfolio-relevant deadlines that often go overlooked: today is the last day to make IRA and HSA contributions for the 2025 tax year, and the first installment of 2026 estimated taxes is also due, a critical consideration for anyone with significant capital gains, freelance income or investment distributions not covered by withholding.

Missing either deadline can mean lost retirement savings opportunities or IRS underpayment penalties, both of which directly affect long-term returns.

Not ready to file?

If you can't complete your return by tonight, don't panic, but do act fast. Taxpayers can get an extension to file their federal income tax return until October 15, but the extension must be requested by April 15 to avoid penalties, according to the IRS.

There are several ways to request an extension:

  • Pay online and select "extension." Taxpayers who make a payment through IRS Direct Pay, debit or credit card or a digital wallet can select "extension" as the reason for the payment, which automatically generates an extension with no additional forms required. Taxpayers should keep their confirmation number for their records.
  • File Form 4868 electronically. Most tax software programs and IRS-authorized e-file providers allow taxpayers to submit Form 4868 online. This is the fastest option and provides confirmation that the IRS accepted the request. Free File and Free Fillable Forms are available to all taxpayers regardless of income.
  • File Form 4868 by mail. Taxpayers can complete and mail Form 4868 to the address listed in the form instructions.

Critical: Extension to file is NOT an extension to pay

This is the most important point for procrastinators to understand. An extension provides extra time to file, not additional time to pay. Taxes owed are still due by April 15.

An approved extension takes care of the failure-to-file penalty, but it does nothing to stop interest from running on what you owe after April 15. The IRS consistently emphasizes that filing an extension, even if you cannot pay in full, is better than failing to file at all, because late-filing penalties are steeper than late-payment penalties.

If you owe but can't pay the full amount today, you should pay what you can now and apply for an online payment plan. Most applicants receive immediate notification of approval or denial, without having to call or write to the IRS.

Special circumstances

Some taxpayers qualify for automatic additional time without needing to file for an extension. Members of the military on duty outside the United States and Puerto Rico receive an automatic two-month extension to file, until June 15, though tax payments are still due April 15 to avoid interest.

Taxpayers serving in combat zones generally have at least 180 days after leaving the combat zone to file returns and pay any taxes due. Taxpayers in certain federally declared disaster areas may also receive additional time to file and pay.

Bottom line

If you're ready, file today. If you're not, request an extension by midnight, but make your best estimate of what you owe and pay it now. The paperwork can wait until October; the bill cannot.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Wednesday: AVGO, MS, HOOD, NEM
data.description:
Stock index futures edged higher ahead of Wednesday’s open, as investors reacted to reports that potential talks with Iran could commence within the next couple of days—boosting confidence in diplomatic progress and supporting a more optimistic market tone. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

Morgan Stanley (MS) shares climbed 2.9% in the premarket session after the firm delivered stronger-than-expected first-quarter results, supported by robust trading activity and continued momentum in its wealth management division. The bank reported Q1 GAAP EPS of $3.43, well above the consensus estimate of $3.02, and up from $2.68 in Q4 2025 and $2.60 in Q1 2025.

Broadcom (AVGO) shares are on the move higher by 2.1% as the company signed a three-year strategic partnership with Meta Platforms (META) aimed at advancing the development of custom artificial intelligence chips and accelerating their deployment across data center infrastructure, the companies announced on Wednesday.

Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares popped 8% after the company expanded its platform to include Kalshi’s prediction markets for users in March 2025. Trading activity in the prediction market space has accelerated sharply in 2026, with volumes on pace to more than quadruple this year and potentially approach $1 trillion within the next four years, according to CNBC.

Newmont Corporation (NEM) shares fell 1.2% in Wednesday’s premarket session after the company announced it had suspended underground operations at its Cadia gold and copper mine in New South Wales, Australia, following overnight seismic activity in the region.

data.title: Voya Financial partners with TELUS Health to expand employee assistance services
data.description:
• Voya Financial (VOYA) on Wednesday said it has partnered with TELUS Health to integrate employee assistance program (EAP) services with its group annual term life insurance offering.
  • The collaboration will provide access to TELUS Health’s EAP platform, combining digital tools and professional support to address mental, emotional, financial and work-life needs.
  • Voya said the move is aimed at enhancing workplace benefits and supporting employee well-being and productivity, with additional services such as mental health programs and critical incident support available at extra cost.
  • VOYA -0.15% premarket to $72.07.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Newmont suspends operations at Cadia gold mine after earthquake
data.description:
Newmont (NEM) down 1.2% pre-market Wednesday after saying it has suspended underground operations at the Cadia gold and copper mine in Australia's New South Wales following seismic activity in the region overnight.

Newmont (NEM) said Australia recorded an earthquake measuring 4.5 on the Local Magnitude scale, which is considered a light to moderate earthquake, and was followed by two aftershock events.

"Underground operations have been paused while specialist teams undertake inspections and assessments," the company said, adding that all personnel were accounted for and returned above ground.

The Cadia operation is one of Australia's largest gold mines, and is currently undergoing a major expansion known as the Panel Caves project; the mine produced 385K oz of gold and 82K tons of copper in 2025, according to Newmont's (NEM) website.

data.title: Traws Pharma prices $60M PIPE financing deal to fund UK flu trial
data.description:
• Traws Pharma has announced a ~$60M private placement PIPE financing, including ~$10M upfront gross proceeds at closing and up to $50M additional capital via milestone-based warrants.
  • The equity was priced at $1.6730 per share, issued via common stock under an “at-the-market” structure compliant with Nasdaq rules.
  • The ~$50M milestone-based warrants include a Series A (~$10.0M) exercisable upon MHRA approval, a Series B (~$10.0M) triggered after shareholder approval, and a Series C (~$30.0M) 3-year warrant exercisable post shareholder approval.
  • Strategic purpose: proceeds will fund advancement of the company’s influenza program via a UK human challenge trial while keeping access to capital aligned with clinical milestones.
  • The financing closed on April 15, 2026, with funding expected on April 16, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Adia Nutrition announces corporate name change to Adia Med
data.description:
• Adia Nutrition (ADIA)  announced on Wednesday that it is in the process of changing its corporate name to Adia Med.
  • The company said that the name change reflects the company's evolving business model, which has shifted significantly toward advanced medical treatments and biologics, as Adia now operates through key divisions, including Adia Med clinics (offering stem cell therapies, platelet-rich plasma (PRP), autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (AHSCT), therapeutic plasma exchange, orthopedic care, pain management, and wound repair) and Adia Labs, which develops and sells biologic products such as AdiaVita and AdiaLink.
  • The company expects the name change process to be completed in the coming months. The ticker symbol (ADIA) is expected to remain unchanged during the transition.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Presidio Production Company declares $0.1013 special dividend
data.description:
• Presidio Production Company (FTW) declares $0.1013/share special dividend.
  • Payable May 18; for shareholders of record May 4; ex-div May 4.
  • See FTW Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Broadcom's deal with Meta boosts confidence in AI ramp: analysts
data.description:
Broadcom's (AVGO) deal with Meta Platforms (META) to provide the Facebook parent with help developing its artificial intelligence chips, known as Meta Training and Inference Accelerators, was praised by Wall Street, as analysts sounded increasingly confident about Broadcom's future.

Broadcom shares rose more than 2% in premarket trading on Wednesday.

“The Meta agreement includes an initial commitment exceeding 1GW of deployment in 2027, representing the first phase of a sustained, multi-gigawatt rollout over time,” J.P. Morgan analyst Harlan Sur wrote in a note to clients. “We expect the first gigawatt of MTIA deployment to occur in 2027, implying $12–$15B of revenue opportunity for Broadcom (including networking), and we believe this further reinforces our conviction that Broadcom can significantly exceed $120B+ of AI revenues in FY27.”

Sur added that the multi-year nature of the deal is a sign that Broadcom will keep benefiting from the “accelerating shift” toward custom chip designs from hyperscalers as they seek to boost performance and efficiency and lower costs. Sur has an Overweight rating and a $500 price target on Broadcom.

Under the deal, Broadcom will deliver technology supporting Meta Training and Inference Accelerator (MTIA) chips through 2029. The initial commitment is more than 1GW and is coming in 2027. 

Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers said the deal “reaffirms” Broadcom's commentary when it reported its fiscal first-quarter results.

“We see the announcement and initial commitment of >1GW as both expected and positive with a long roadmap and a multi-GW rollout,” Rakers posited. “The multi-GW roadmap and explicit commitment to use AVGO across design, packaging, and networking show Broadcom integrating deeper into the AI stack across the industry. Meta will begin deploying four new MTIA generations in two years. We continue to expect hyperscalers to leverage a multi-vendor accelerator strategy.”

Rakers, who has an Overweight rating and $430 price target on Broadcom, also noted that expanding into inference is “key” to boosting Broadcom's total addressable market.

“We see ASICs and CPUs taking a more important role as agentic AI usage grows,” Rakers added. “Meta's plan to use an iterative approach with a six-month chip cadence should be positive for Broadcom w/ MTIA 400/450/500 all using the same networking infrastructure. We expect Tomahawk/Jericho to be more sticky/entrenched within Meta's infrastructure ecosystem despite some added execution risk.”

data.title: FRMO Non-GAAP EPS of $0.38, revenue of $0.86M
data.description:
• FRMO press release (FRMO): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.38.
  • Revenue of $0.86M (-61.4% Y/Y).

  • Consulting and advisory fees of $0.9 million for the third quarter of 2026, a decrease of 61% from 2025.

  • Other income, net of $308.4 million for the third quarter of 2026, an increase of 481% from 2025.
